page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 80 dedicated virtual CPUs, which correspond to 40 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 320 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities across multiple domains. The server achieves a Passmark CPU score of 69,828.6 and a multi-core stress-ng CPU performance score of 107,964.98, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Cryptographic execution is a notable strength, with the Passmark CPU encryption test reaching 85,389.14, placing it in the top 10% tier. Memory performance is also robust, demonstrating a read-only large block bandwidth of 200.15 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 123.81 GB/sec.

Given its balanced resource allocation and strong multi-core performance, this server is suited for database operations, static web serving, and moderate computational tasks. It achieves 77.46 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 47.97 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated AMD EPYC hardware provides high resource density and strong multi-threaded performance without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances, though the lack of local NVMe storage requires external storage attachments for persistent data.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 is a general-purpose server configured with 80 dedicated vCPUs and 320 GB of RAM, utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. This configuration provides a balanced 4 GB of memory per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 107,964.98 and a Passmark CPU score of 69,828.6. Cryptographic performance is particularly high, ranking in the top 10% with a Passmark encryption score of 85,389.14. Additionally, the instance demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and database operation capabilities, making it highly effective for enterprise web serving, database management, and multi-threaded processing workloads. It offers a balanced, cost-efficient profile for resource-intensive general-purpose applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 is a general-purpose server featuring 80 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 320 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core performance suited for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 features 80 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 320 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
tagline: A balanced multi-core server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-bandwidth memory for demanding general-purpose and database workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
320 GB of system memory providing 4 GB per vCPU
Top-tier cryptographic performance and strong multi-core processing
High-bandwidth memory performance exceeding 200 GB/sec for large blocks
Optimized for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded workloads
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
